items.description: On July 21, 356 BCE, one of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World was destroyed in an act of deliberate vandalism. Test your knowledge of the monument, its history, and its enduring legacy

items.title: Tate brothers appear in US court for extradition proceedings
items.description: They were arrested on Saturday after the UK announced dozens of new charges, including rape and sex trafficking, which they deny.

items.title: Can journalism reinvent itself before AI completely upends it?
items.description: What will a trusted, sustainable, global news organisation look like in an era of agentic AI? And can we reinvent ourselves before someone else builds a less trustworthy, more dangerous alternative?

items.title: Ozempic maker sues US rival over ad campaign
items.description: Novo Nordisk has claimed that Eli Lilly is using bad data to compare the companies' weight-loss and diabetes products in its ads.

items.title: Tunisia reels in extreme heat as temperatures near 50°C
items.description: An exceptionally intense heat wave swept across Tunisia, pushing temperatures to nearly 50 degrees Celsius and breaking historical records in multiple regions, local media reported on Saturday

items.title: Gabon's President Oligui Ngema arrives in France for state visit
items.description: Gabon's President Brice Oligui Ngema kicked off a state visit to France on Monday, a follow up to French President Macron's visit to Libreville last year.

items.title: Andy Burnham becomes U.K. PM, promising new social and political model
items.description: ‘I go with good grace and a smile on my face’, says outgoing Prime Minister Keir Starmer says

items.title: India extends grant support to Maldives for fitness centres across 42 islands
items.description: On July 19, Memoranda of Understanding (MoUs) were signed by Maldivian Foreign Minister Iruthisham Adam, Indian High Commissioner G Balasubramanian and Maldives' Youth Empowerment, Sports and Fitness Minister Abdulla Rafiu at a ceremony in Male
